title: Dashboards and their SQL blocks
description: Benchling Insights Dashboards and their SQL blocks.

items.title: Dashboard and its SQL blocks
items.description: A Benchling Insights Dashboard containing one or more SQL query blocks.

Project.description: Name of the Benchling project this dashboard belongs to.

API ID.description: API ID for the dashboard created during an export operation. This is ignored for import operations.

Parameters.description: Dashboard parameters. Not populated by export (not currently exposed by the Benchling API); defined for forward-compatibility.

items.title: Dashboard parameter
items.description: A parameter that can be referenced in the dashboard's SQL queries using the {{param}} placeholder.

Name.description: Parameter name as displayed in the dashboard UI. This is the value that will be substituted into the SQL query using the {{param}} placeholder.

Type.description: The parameter's type controls which values it accepts and how those values are substituted into the SQL query.

Definition.description: Name of the target object if Type is Dropdown, Entity, Inventory, Study, Workflow task group, or Workflow task. Use 'All' to allow all instances of the type.

Multi-select.description: Whether the parameter allows multiple values to be selected at once.

Blocks.description: SQL query blocks within the dashboard, in display (reading) order.

items.title: SQL block
items.description: A single SQL query block in the dashboard.

Name.description: Block name as displayed in the dashboard UI.

SQL.description: The SQL query for this block. May be a filename (e.g. 'Block_1.sql') referencing a file in the same dashboard directory, or an inline SQL string using the YAML pipe ('|') operator. Best practice is to use a separate .sql file.

Chart.description: Chart display configuration. Not populated by export (not currently exposed by the Benchling API); defined for forward-compatibility.

Type.description: The chart type used to display query results.

Parameters.description: Free-form string describing chart configuration (axes, grouping, colors, etc.). Intentionally unstructured until Benchling exposes chart config in the API.
